            % Find common elements between mix and sensitivities
            [common_elements, idx_mix, idx_sens] = intersect(element_names_mix, sensitivity_element_names, 'stable');
{Unrecognized function or variable 'element_names_mix'.

            intensity_mix_i = app.base.project.session.spot.bkgd_corrected_cps_mix{unk,:}; % Iimix
            element_names_mix = app.base.project.session.spot.bkgd_corrected_cps_mix.Properties.VariableNames;

            % Get sensitivity table and its column names
            sensitivities = app.base.project.session.spot.sensitivities{bracket_idx,prm_idx}(unk,:);
            sensitivity_element_names = sensitivities.Properties.VariableNames;

            % Find common elements between mix and sensitivities
            [common_elements, idx_mix, idx_sens] = intersect(element_names_mix, sensitivity_element_names, 'stable');

            % Filter intensity_mix_i to only include elements present in sensitivities
            intensity_mix_filtered = intensity_mix_i(idx_mix);

            % Find the most intense signal from the filtered data
            [max_intensity, max_col_idx_filtered] = max(intensity_mix_filtered);
            is_mix_name = common_elements{max_col_idx_filtered};
            is_mix_intensity = max_intensity;
            is_mix_idx = idx_mix(max_col_idx_filtered); % Index in original mix table

            % Calculate the sensitivity of all analytes relative to the
            % sensitivity of the assumed internal standard in the mix
            sensitivity_is_mix = sensitivities.(is_mix_name);
            sensitivity_relative_to_is_mix = table2array(sensitivities) ./ sensitivity_is_mix;

            % Calculate the concentration ratio of all elements relative to
            % the internal standard in the mix (only for common elements)
            conc_ratio = (intensity_mix_filtered ./ is_mix_intensity) ./ sensitivity_relative_to_is_mix(idx_sens);

      % cps like in SILLS

            intensity_mix_i = app.base.project.session.spot.bkgd_corrected_cps_mix{unk,:}; % Iimix
            element_names_mix = app.base.project.session.spot.bkgd_corrected_cps_mix.Properties.VariableNames;

            % Get sensitivity table and its column names
            sensitivities = app.base.project.session.spot.sensitivities{bracket_idx,prm_idx}(unk,:);
            sensitivity_element_names = sensitivities.Properties.VariableNames;

            % Find common elements between mix and sensitivities
            [common_elements, idx_mix, idx_sens] = intersect(element_names_mix, sensitivity_element_names, 'stable');

            % Filter intensity_mix_i to only include elements present in sensitivities
            intensity_mix_filtered = intensity_mix_i(idx_mix);

            % Find the most intense signal from the filtered data
            [max_intensity, max_col_idx_filtered] = max(intensity_mix_filtered);
            is_mix_name = common_elements{max_col_idx_filtered};
            is_mix_intensity = max_intensity;
            is_mix_idx = idx_mix(max_col_idx_filtered); % Index in original mix table

            % Calculate the sensitivity of all analytes relative to the
            % sensitivity of the assumed internal standard in the mix
            sensitivity_is_mix = sensitivities.(is_mix_name);
            sensitivity_relative_to_is_mix = table2array(sensitivities) ./ sensitivity_is_mix;

            % Calculate the concentration ratio of all elements relative to
            % the internal standard in the mix
            conc_ratio_mix = (intensity_mix_filtered ./ is_mix_intensity) ./ sensitivity_relative_to_is_mix(idx_sens);

incl_fe_ratio

incl_fe_ratio =

     1



            % After calculating conc_ratio_mix and before using it further:

            % Handle Fe ratio if Fe is present
            fe_idx = find(strcmp(common_elements, 'Fe57')); % Find Fe57 index

            if ~isempty(fe_idx) && ~isnan(incl_fe_ratio) && incl_fe_ratio > 0
                % incl_fe_ratio is defined as FeO/(FeO + Fe2O3)
                a = incl_fe_ratio;

                % Split Fe concentration ratio into FeO and Fe2O3 components
                % Based on SILLS formula:
                % FeO component (Fe2+):
                fe2_fraction = 1 - (1/(1 + (0.699480/0.777314)*((1-a)/a)));

                % Fe2O3 component (Fe3+):
                fe3_fraction = 1/(1 + (0.699480/0.777314)*((1-a)/a));

                % Adjust the Fe concentration ratio
                % Store original Fe ratio
                fe_total = conc_ratio_mix(fe_idx);

                % You might want to store both separately or adjust the single Fe value
                % Option 1: Adjust in place (keeping only total as FeO equivalent)
                conc_ratio_mix(fe_idx) = fe_total * fe2_fraction;

                % Option 2: If you have separate FeO and Fe2O3 columns (uncomment if needed)
                % fe2_idx = find(strcmp(common_elements, 'FeO'));
                % fe3_idx = find(strcmp(common_elements, 'Fe2O3'));
                % if ~isempty(fe2_idx)
                %     conc_ratio_mix(fe2_idx) = fe_total * fe2_fraction;
                % end
                % if ~isempty(fe3_idx)
                %     conc_ratio_mix(fe3_idx) = fe_total * fe3_fraction;
                % end
            end

app.base.project.session.oxides.preferred_isotopes

    {'Ti49'  }


            % After calculating conc_ratio_mix and before using it further:

            % Handle Fe ratio if Fe is present
            % First check if there's a preferred Fe isotope
            preferred_isotopes = app.base.project.session.oxides.preferred_isotopes;
            fe_preferred = preferred_isotopes(contains(preferred_isotopes, 'Fe'));

            if ~isempty(fe_preferred) && ~isempty(fe_preferred{1})
                % Use the preferred Fe isotope
                fe_idx = find(strcmp(common_elements, fe_preferred{1}));
            else
                % Fallback: find any Fe isotope and use the first one
                fe_idx = find(contains(common_elements, 'Fe'));
                if ~isempty(fe_idx)
                    fe_idx = fe_idx(1);
                end
            end

            if ~isempty(fe_idx) && ~isnan(incl_fe_ratio) && incl_fe_ratio > 0
                % incl_fe_ratio is defined as FeO/(FeO + Fe2O3)
                a = incl_fe_ratio;

                % Split Fe concentration ratio into FeO and Fe2O3 components
                % Based on SILLS formula:
                % FeO component (Fe2+):
                fe2_fraction = 1 - (1/(1 + (0.699480/0.777314)*((1-a)/a)));

                % Fe2O3 component (Fe3+):
                fe3_fraction = 1/(1 + (0.699480/0.777314)*((1-a)/a));

                % Adjust the Fe concentration ratio
                % Store original Fe ratio
                fe_total = conc_ratio_mix(fe_idx);

                % Adjust in place (keeping only total as FeO equivalent)
                conc_ratio_mix(fe_idx) = fe_total * fe2_fraction;
            end
conc_ratio_mix(fe_idx)

ans =

  <a href="matlab:helpPopup single" style="font-weight:bold">single</a>

     0

 Handle Fe ratio if Fe is present
{Unrecognized function or variable 'Handle'.
} 

            % Handle Fe ratio if Fe is present
            % First check if there's a preferred Fe isotope
            preferred_isotopes = app.base.project.session.oxides.preferred_isotopes;
            fe_preferred_cell = preferred_isotopes(contains(preferred_isotopes, 'Fe'));

            if ~isempty(fe_preferred_cell) && ~isempty(fe_preferred_cell{1})
                % Use the preferred Fe isotope
                fe_idx = find(strcmp(common_elements, fe_preferred_cell{1}));
            else
                % Fallback: find any Fe isotope and use the first one
                fe_idx = find(contains(common_elements, 'Fe'));
                if ~isempty(fe_idx)
                    fe_idx = fe_idx(1);
                end
            end

            % Apply Fe ratio correction if Fe exists and ratio is valid
            if ~isempty(fe_idx) && ~isnan(incl_fe_ratio) && incl_fe_ratio ~= 0 && incl_fe_ratio ~= 1
                % incl_fe_ratio is defined as FeO/(FeO + Fe2O3)
                fe_ratio = incl_fe_ratio;

                % Get total Fe concentration ratio
                fe_total = conc_ratio_mix(fe_idx);

                % Calculate Fe2O3 and FeO fractions using the same formula as in total_oxide_calculation
                % 0.699480/0.777314 is the ratio of Fe in Fe2O3 vs FeO
                fe_constant = 0.699480/0.777314;

                % Calculate fractions
                fe2o3_fraction = fe_total * (1-(1/(1+(fe_constant)*((1-fe_ratio)/fe_ratio))));
                feo_fraction = fe_total * (1/(1+(fe_constant)*((1-fe_ratio)/fe_ratio)));

                % Update Fe concentration ratio to FeO component
                conc_ratio_mix(fe_idx) = feo_fraction;

                % Add Fe2O3 component
                conc_ratio_mix = [conc_ratio_mix, fe2o3_fraction];
                common_elements = [common_elements, {'Fe2O3'}];
            end
fe2o3_fraction
{Unrecognized function or variable 'fe2o3_fraction'.
} 

            % in the HOST_CALC file the oxide_mat is either taken form the
            % real value if the host was also quantified using total oxides
            % or it is simply recalculated if not
            if ~isequal(app.base.project.session.spot.internal_standard{bracket_idx,prm_idx}{unk}, 'Total Oxides')
                % in this case, we have to recalculate the total oxide wt%
                % from the composition

                 mass_fraction_host_i = app.base.project.session.spot.matrix_composition{bracket_idx,prm_idx}{unk,:}; % Cihost

                 % Get oxide conversion factors
                 oxide_factors = app.base.project.session.oxides.conversion_factors;

                 % Get the element names (column names from the table)
                 element_names = app.base.project.session.spot.matrix_composition{bracket_idx,prm_idx}.Properties.VariableNames;

                 % Initialize total oxide wt%
                 total_oxide_wt = 0;

                 % Loop through each element
                 for j = 1:length(element_names)
                     element = element_names{j};
                     mass_frac_ugg = mass_fraction_host_i(j);

                     % Skip if mass fraction is zero or empty
                     if mass_frac_ugg == 0 || isnan(mass_frac_ugg)
                         continue;
                     end

                     % Extract base element name (letters only)
                     base_element = regexp(element, '^[A-Za-z]+', 'match');

                     if ~isempty(base_element) && isKey(oxide_factors, base_element{1})
                         % Convert µg/g to wt% (divide by 10000)
                         element_wt_percent = mass_frac_ugg / 10000;

                         % Convert element wt% to oxide wt%
                         oxide_wt_percent = element_wt_percent * oxide_factors(base_element{1});

                         % Add to total
                         total_oxide_wt = total_oxide_wt + oxide_wt_percent;
                     end
                 end

                 % total_oxide_wt now contains the total oxide wt% for this unknown
                 host_total_oxide = total_oxide_wt;


            else % in this case the host is also quantified using total oxides and we can just take the value from there

                host_total_oxide = app.base.project.session.spot.internal_standard_oxide_wt{bracket_idx,prm_idx}{unk}; %Oxide_mat
                host_fe_ratio = app.base.project.session.spot.Fe_ratio_for_total_oxide{bracket_idx,prm_idx}{unk}; % fe_ratio

            end


            second_internal_standard_name = app.base.project.session.spot.second_internal_standard_inclusion{bracket_idx,prm_idx}{unk}; % is
            second_internal_standard_mass_fraction = app.base.project.session.spot.second_internal_standard_mass_fraction_inclusion{bracket_idx,prm_idx}{unk}; % Cisinc
            sensitivity_relative_to_is2 = app.sensitivity_relative_to_second_internal_standard_inclusion{bracket_idx,prm_idx}{unk,:};

            incl_total_oxide = app.base.project.session.spot.internal_standard_oxide_wt_inclusion{bracket_idx,prm_idx}{unk}; % Oxide_inc
            incl_fe_ratio = app.base.project.session.spot.Fe_ratio_for_total_oxide_inclusion{bracket_idx,prm_idx}{unk}; % fe_ratio

            % define the internal standard as the element with the highes
            % cps like in SILLS

            intensity_mix_i = app.base.project.session.spot.bkgd_corrected_cps_mix{unk,:}; % Iimix
            element_names_mix = app.base.project.session.spot.bkgd_corrected_cps_mix.Properties.VariableNames;

            % Get sensitivity table and its column names
            sensitivities = app.base.project.session.spot.sensitivities{bracket_idx,prm_idx}(unk,:);
            sensitivity_element_names = sensitivities.Properties.VariableNames;

            % Find common elements between mix and sensitivities
            [common_elements, idx_mix, idx_sens] = intersect(element_names_mix, sensitivity_element_names, 'stable');

            % Filter intensity_mix_i to only include elements present in sensitivities
            intensity_mix_filtered = intensity_mix_i(idx_mix);

            % Find the most intense signal from the filtered data
            [max_intensity, max_col_idx_filtered] = max(intensity_mix_filtered);
            is_mix_name = common_elements{max_col_idx_filtered};
            is_mix_intensity = max_intensity;
            is_mix_idx = idx_mix(max_col_idx_filtered); % Index in original mix table

            % Calculate the sensitivity of all analytes relative to the
            % sensitivity of the assumed internal standard in the mix
            sensitivity_is_mix = sensitivities.(is_mix_name);
            sensitivity_relative_to_is_mix = table2array(sensitivities) ./ sensitivity_is_mix;

            % Calculate the concentration ratio of all elements relative to
            % the internal standard in the mix
            conc_ratio_mix = (intensity_mix_filtered ./ is_mix_intensity) ./ sensitivity_relative_to_is_mix(idx_sens);



            % After calculating conc_ratio_mix and before using it further:

            % Handle Fe ratio if Fe is present
            % First check if there's a preferred Fe isotope
            preferred_isotopes = app.base.project.session.oxides.preferred_isotopes;
            fe_preferred_cell = preferred_isotopes(contains(preferred_isotopes, 'Fe'));

            if ~isempty(fe_preferred_cell) && ~isempty(fe_preferred_cell{1})
                % Use the preferred Fe isotope
                fe_idx = find(strcmp(common_elements, fe_preferred_cell{1}));
            else
                % Fallback: find any Fe isotope and use the first one
                fe_idx = find(contains(common_elements, 'Fe'));
                if ~isempty(fe_idx)
                    fe_idx = fe_idx(1);
                end
            end

            % Apply Fe ratio correction if Fe exists and ratio is valid
            if ~isempty(fe_idx) && ~isnan(incl_fe_ratio) && incl_fe_ratio ~= 0 && incl_fe_ratio ~= 1
                % incl_fe_ratio is defined as FeO/(FeO + Fe2O3)
                fe_ratio = incl_fe_ratio;

                % Get total Fe concentration ratio
                fe_total = conc_ratio_mix(fe_idx);

                % Calculate Fe2O3 and FeO fractions using the same formula as in total_oxide_calculation
                % 0.699480/0.777314 is the ratio of Fe in Fe2O3 vs FeO
                fe_constant = 0.699480/0.777314;

                % Calculate fractions
                fe2o3_fraction = fe_total * (1-(1/(1+(fe_constant)*((1-fe_ratio)/fe_ratio))));
                feo_fraction = fe_total * (1/(1+(fe_constant)*((1-fe_ratio)/fe_ratio)));

                % Update Fe concentration ratio to FeO component
                conc_ratio_mix(fe_idx) = feo_fraction;

                % Add Fe2O3 component
                conc_ratio_mix = [conc_ratio_mix, fe2o3_fraction];
                common_elements = [common_elements, {'Fe2O3'}];
            end
